notes: Runs against the LIVE FM Notion board read-only: upserts must use --dry-run (captured to outputs/upsert-dry-run.txt), comments are written to outputs/estimation-comment.md instead of posted.

evals.prompt: We just clarified FM-49. Estimate it — give it points based on effort, split developer points and QA points, and put the estimate on the ticket.
evals.expected_output: Reads FM-49 and 5-10 Done tickets that have an Effort level set (FM-39/37/35/34/33/32/31/30/23) to calibrate; estimates Dev and QA points separately with 2-3 named comparables; sets the effort property using the board's observed scale (Small/Medium/Large) via upsert --dry-run; writes a structured estimation comment (Dev points, QA points, total, comparables, confidence) to estimation-comment.md.
assertions: Calibrates before estimating: shows evidence of reading the effort value of at least 3 Done tickets from the board before producing the estimate
Produces SEPARATE Developer points and QA points, each with a stated effort-based reason
Sets the effort property using the board's observed scale value (Small / Medium / Large) — not an invented scale — via upsert-ticket-details.sh with --dry-run
Names at least 2 comparable tickets by key (FM-xx) that justify the estimate
Touches ONLY the effort property: no status/title/priority/description/body change; breakdown lives in the comment text, not the ticket body

evals.prompt: Size FM-24 please — it needs an effort estimate before the development planner picks it up. I want both dev and QA points on it.
evals.expected_output: Same calibrated flow for a larger UI-alignment polish ticket (multi-element Figma alignment, likely Medium vs the mostly-Small reference set); Dev/QA split with comparables and a scale-consistent effort value.
assertions: Calibrates before estimating: shows evidence of reading the effort value of at least 3 Done tickets from the board before producing the estimate
Produces SEPARATE Developer points and QA points, each with a stated effort-based reason
Sets the effort property using the board's observed scale value (Small / Medium / Large) via upsert-ticket-details.sh with --dry-run
Names at least 2 comparable tickets by key (FM-xx) that justify the estimate
QA estimate reasoning reflects the ticket's actual test burden (multi-element UI alignment against a Figma frame, Android+iOS visual verification) rather than restating the dev reasoning
Touches ONLY the effort property: no status/title/priority/description/body change; breakdown lives in the comment text, not the ticket body

evals.prompt: Estimate FM-29 and set its points.
evals.expected_output: FM-29 (Add Pet Success Screen) has no body/acceptance criteria. The skill should refuse to put a number on an unclarified spec: no effort write, recommend running /clarifying-ticket FM-29 first (or at minimum flag missing AC and low confidence prominently rather than silently estimating).
assertions: Detects that FM-29 has no acceptance criteria / spec body and says so explicitly
Does not silently put a confident number on the unclarified ticket: either declines to set the effort property, or at minimum marks the estimate low-confidence with explicit assumptions
Recommends clarifying the ticket first (e.g. via /clarifying-ticket FM-29) before or alongside any estimate
Never posts/sets anything beyond the effort property — no status/title/body change in any dry-run payload
